message:
fix(abi): prevent reentrant TLS panic in dlerror and add startup guard to runtime policy

The dlfcn dlerror thread-local state used RefCell which panics on
reentrant borrow_mut(). During early startup, dlsym → set_dlerror →
TLS init → dlsym → clear_dlerror creates a reentrant access chain.
Replace RefCell<Option<Vec<u8>>> with Cell<*const u8> using static
string pointers — this is reentry-safe and avoids heap allocation
since all error messages are &'static [u8].

Also add a RUNTIME_READY atomic guard to runtime_policy::decide() so
that the full kernel decision path is only used after initialization
completes, preventing deadlocks when TLS/heap/runtime state aren't
ready during early startup.

Additional changes:
- Remove unused LargeAllocator and record_allocator_stats from malloc
  allocator (dead code after prior refactoring)
- Remove unused Galois imports from string_abi
- Add memcmp and strcmp benchmarks to string_bench with corresponding
  entries in symbol_latency_capture_map.v1.json

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
